data.title: NETSCOUT honored by Frost & Sullivan as 2025 Global Company of the Year for network monitoring, recognized for leadership and innovation.
data.description: NETSCOUT, based in San Antonio, has been recognized for its outstanding performance in network monitoring, performance assurance, and real-time visibility. The company has demonstrated market leadership, innovation, and customer-centric execution. On January 13, 2026, Frost & Sullivan announced that NETSCOUT received the prestigious 2025 award.

data.title: BlueMatrix and Perplexity are partnering to introduce AI-powered discovery to institutional research, enhancing efficiency and effectiveness.
data.description: Two tech companies, BlueMatrix and Perplexity, have joined forces to introduce AI-driven discovery tools for institutional research. The collaboration between the firms, based in Durham, North Carolina, and San Francisco, respectively, aims to leverage artificial intelligence to enhance the research process. This partnership signifies a significant move in the industry, combining the expertise of BlueMatrix and Perplexity to offer advanced solutions in institutional research.

data.title: Income Lab launches AI Scribe to make retirement plans from client conversations. Zoom meetings are listened to and info is turned into plans.
data.description: Income Laboratory, Inc. unveiled a groundbreaking AI Scribe tool as part of its retirement planning software offerings. This innovative capability automatically converts real-time client discussions into retirement plans. Through secure monitoring of Zoom meetings, AI Scribe extracts crucial information to create or revise an Income Lab plan. This advancement enables advisors to concentrate on client interactions while the tool processes significant data. Discover this cutting-edge technology from Income Laboratory, Inc.

data.title: GOP lawmakers warn Powell probe may impact markets, push rates higher. Sen. Thom Tillis opposes Fed nominee until investigation is resolved. Sen. John Kennedy criticizes probe, predicting higher rates. Senate Majority Leader John Thune and Speaker Mike Johnson watching the situation unfold.
data.description: The Department of Justice's criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell has raised worries among President Donald Trump's allies and officials. Senator Thom Tillis from North Carolina declared he will reject any Fed nominee Trump proposes until the legal issue is resolved. This stance could potentially prevent a nominee from advancing to a full Senate vote, given the tight split in the Senate Banking Committee. Senator John Kennedy from Louisiana criticized the investigation, cautioning that a public clash between the White House and the Federal Reserve might drive interest rates up instead of down, labeling such a confrontation as unnecessary and unproductive. Meanwhile, Senate Majority Leader John Thune from South Dakota and Speaker Mike Johnson from Louisiana, while expressing concerns about the investigation, advocate for allowing the investigation to unfold.

data.title: Black Rifle Coffee Company previews 2025 fiscal year with projected revenue of $395M, 35% gross margin, and $20M EBITDA.
data.description: In Salt Lake City, Black Rifle Coffee Company (NYSE: BRCC), a premium beverage company founded by Veterans, is scheduled to present at the 28th Annual ICR Conference. They have disclosed preliminary and unaudited results for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2025. The Company had earlier estimated fiscal year 2025 revenues to be a minimum of $395 million, with a gross margin of at least 35%, and an Adjusted EBITDA of at least $20 million.

data.title: VF Corp to attend the 28th Annual ICR Conference. Exciting updates expected. Save the date!
data.description: The number of COVID-19 cases continues to rise, with a total of 250,000 confirmed cases worldwide. In the US alone, there have been 50,000 reported cases, prompting strict stay-at-home orders in many states. The death toll has reached 10,000 globally, with over 1,000 deaths in the US. Health officials are urging the public to practice social distancing and good hygiene to prevent further spread of the virus. In response, governments are implementing various measures to support healthcare systems and protect citizens.

data.title: Kian Capital invests in Eden Brothers, appoints Michael Hollenstein as CEO, specialized in flower seeds and bulbs.
data.description: In Atlanta and Charlotte, Kian Capital and its subsidiary, Eden Brothers, have welcomed Michael Hollenstein as the new Chief Executive Officer. Eden Brothers is a rapidly expanding e-commerce retailer that focuses on providing flower seeds and bulbs to gardening enthusiasts across the country. Hollenstein, a seasoned executive with more than two decades of experience in product management and merchandising, will be taking the helm. His appointment brings a wealth of expertise in driving growth and sustainability to the company.

data.title: Emera Inc. declares quarterly dividends: $0.7325 per common share; $0.3094 Series A First Preferred Share; $0.40213 Series C First Preferred Share; $0.28125 Series E First Preferred Share.
data.description: In Halifax, Nova Scotia, on January 13, 2026, Emera Inc. (TSX/NYSE: EMA) announced the declaration of quarterly dividends for its common shares and First Preferred Shares. The dividends are set to be paid on or after February 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 30, 2026. The declared amounts are as follows: $0.7325 per common share, $0.3094 per Series A First Preferred Share, $0.40213 per Series C First Preferred Share, and $0.28125 per Series E First Preferred Share.

data.title: L3Harris partners with DoW for missile solutions, $1 billion proposed investment to boost rocket motor production.
data.description: In an unprecedented move, L3Harris Technologies (NYSE: LHX) revealed plans for a groundbreaking collaboration with the Department of War to enhance production capabilities for solid rocket motors used in critical U.S. and allied missiles. The proposed partnership involves a substantial investment by the Department of War in L3Harris’ Missile Solutions division through a $1 billion convertible preferred security. This investment is structured to convert into common equity upon an initial public offering.

data.title: American Pride Bank expands One-Time Close Construction Programs via APB Wholesale division in ATL, boosting investors' options.
data.description: In Atlanta on January 13, 2026, American Pride Bank (APB) revealed the launch of an enhanced line of One-Time Close Construction Loan offerings via its wholesale mortgage arm, APB Wholesale. This move signifies APB's dedication to offering adaptable financial solutions and marks a significant development in its services.
KAZAKHSTAN'S OIL AND GAS CONDENSATE PRODUCTION DECLINES BY 35% IN JANUARY 1-12 FROM DECEMBER'S AVERAGE TO 1.21 MILLION BPD, SOURCES

data.title: Maple Leaf Foods forecasts mid-single digit revenue growth in 2026, with Adjusted EBITDA of $520-540 million and a 10% dividend increase.
data.description: Maple Leaf Foods Inc. announced its 2026 financial guidance, highlighting a mid-single digit increase in revenue from 2025. They expect adjusted EBITDA to be approximately $520-$540 million, aiming to maintain an investment-grade balance sheet. The company plans to invest $160-$180 million in capital, focusing on productivity enhancement and technology. Additionally, Maple Leaf Foods will increase its quarterly dividend by approximately 10%. CEO Curtis Frank expressed confidence in their growth, margin expansion, and ability to generate strong free cash flow. These strategies aim to create sustained growth and value for shareholders.

data.title: First Quantum to announce Q4 & year-end 2025 results on Feb 10 after TSX closure, with conference call on Feb 11 at 9:00 am ET.
data.description: In Toronto on January 13, 2026, First Quantum Minerals Ltd. announced that they will be revealing their financial and operating outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on February 10, 2026, following the closure of the Toronto Stock Exchange. The company, listed as TSX: FM, is set to hold a conference call and webcast elaborating on these results on February 11, 2026, at 9:00 am (ET). Moreover, they will also disclose preliminary production figures for 2025. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Unity Bancorp reported quarterly earnings of $15.5 million and full-year earnings of $58.0 million, showing growth from the previous year. CEO stated that the company achieved a 39.8% increase in net income and continued to expand across loans and deposits.
data.description: Unity Bancorp, Inc., parent company of Unity Bank, reported a net income of $15.5 million, or $1.52 per diluted share, for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, up from $14.4 million in the previous quarter. For the twelve months ended December 31, 2025, they reported a net income of $58.0 million, compared to $41.5 million in the previous year. This represents a 39.8% increase in net income. CEO James A. Hughes noted a record year for the company, with a 2.17% Return on Assets (ROA) and 18.07% Return on Equity (ROE). Gross loans increased by $284 million, deposits grew by $224 million, and customer deposits increased by $168 million. The company emphasized organic growth strategies and expressed optimism moving into 2026.

data.title: London-based SaaS company Diginius becomes a TikTok Channel Sales Partner, gaining a Marketing Technology badge.
data.description: Diginius, a London SaaS company, shared exciting news on Jan. 13, 2026, stating that they have attained a Marketing Technology badge within the TikTok Marketing Partners Program. This prestigious recognition places them in the top tier of marketing technology providers in TikTok's program.
